name: RAGTruth++
venue: arXiv-only / blog release (no peer-reviewed venue found as of May 2026). Published as HuggingFace dataset + Blue Guardrails blog post.
authors: Miriam Kummel and Mathis (founders, Blue Guardrails GmbH)
arxiv_id: [uncertain] — no arxiv paper found. Dataset released at https://huggingface.co/datasets/blue-guardrails/ragtruth-plus-plus with accompanying blog post at https://www.blueguardrails.com/en/blog/ragtruth-plus-plus-enhanced-hallucination-detection-benchmark. No formal arXiv ID identified.
dataset_size: 408 prompt-response pairs (a random subset of the original RAGTruth test set's 1,800 QA and summarization examples); 865 annotated hallucination spans (up from 86 in the original annotations for this subset — a 1,005.8% increase)
annotation_unit: Text spans with character-level start/end positions, label (hallucination type), and free-text reasoning field per span. Same span-level unit as RAGTruth but with substantially denser annotation coverage — average 2.12 hallucination spans per response vs. 0.21 in original RAGTruth for this subset.
ground_truth_source: Hybrid: (1) automated detection using Blue Guardrails' proprietary hallucination detection system as a first pass; (2) two independent human annotators reviewed every automated prediction, confirmed/rejected/adjusted spans, and added missing hallucinations; (3) consensus adjudication resolving 310 disputed spans. Inter-annotator F1: 0.78 (substantial agreement).
judge_model: Blue Guardrails proprietary automated detection model used as first-pass oracle (model identity not disclosed); final labels are human-adjudicated.
domain: QA (234 examples) and Summarization (174 examples). Same domains as RAGTruth QA/Summarization splits: general-knowledge QA and news summarization. Topics include cooking, history, product information, entertainment, politics, health. Data-to-text task excluded from this subset.
context_length_range: [uncertain] — not separately reported for the 408-sample subset. Inherits from RAGTruth QA and Summarization tasks: QA contexts mean 243 tokens (max 509), summarization contexts mean ~370–648 tokens (max 1,749). Short-to-medium context, same as RAGTruth.
error_taxonomy: Five span-level labels (expanded from RAGTruth's four): (1) Evident Conflict — direct contradiction with source; (2) Subtle Conflict — contextual divergence altering meaning; (3) Evident Baseless Info — fabricated details with no source evidence; (4) Subtle Baseless Info — inferred or assumed details beyond source; (5) Incorrect Refusal — refusing to answer when source information is available (new category vs. original RAGTruth). Also includes per-span 'reasoning' field explaining why the span is a hallucination.
contamination_risk: medium — subset of publicly-released RAGTruth test set. Static set; responses generated by GPT-3.5/GPT-4/Llama-2/Mistral in 2023. Same contamination risk as RAGTruth for newer models.
license: [uncertain] — not explicitly stated on HuggingFace dataset card or blog post. No license file found in the dataset repository. Assumed non-commercial/research use only based on Blue Guardrails commercial context, but this is unconfirmed.
annotation_cost_per_sample: [uncertain] — not reported. Two-annotator + adjudication process on 408 responses suggests substantial human effort. Blog reports 310 disputed spans were adjudicated. Automated first pass reduces cost vs. pure manual annotation.
reported_value: RT4CHART (Retromorphic Testing, arXiv 2603.27752) achieves F1 0.776 on RAGTruth++, outperforming strongest baseline by 83%. Hallucination rate in this set: 74.75% of responses contain at least one hallucination span (vs. 15.9% in original RAGTruth annotations for same subset).
overlapping_benchmark: None directly — QA and Summarization tasks, not matching our MATH-500/GSM8K/GPQA/HotpotQA/TriviaQA/WebQ benchmarks
model_used_in_paper: Responses from: Llama-2-7B-chat, Llama-2-13B-chat, Llama-2-70B-chat, Mistral-7B-Instruct, GPT-3.5-turbo-0613, GPT-4-0613 (inherited from RAGTruth source)
applicability: high — addresses the known false-negative problem in RAGTruth; 10x denser span annotations make it a harder and more reliable benchmark; directly suitable for evaluating span-level hallucination detectors in RAG settings. Small size (408) makes it feasible for quick pilot.
preemption_risk: low — this is a re-annotated evaluation corpus, not a detection method. No spectral, frequency-domain, or entropy-based methodology involved.
bracha_alignment: Moderate — the dense binary/span labels with high hallucination rate (74.75%) and small size (408) are well-suited for conformal calibration experiments (LTT/COIN). The realistic hallucination rate (vs. the original 15.9%) gives more reliable calibration targets.
ofir_alignment: Low — pure evaluation dataset. Relevant only as a testbed; no spectral or kernel methods involved in the dataset itself.
notes: Key contribution: reveals that RAGTruth's original annotations have a severe false-negative problem — 86 spans annotated in the original vs. 865 found by RAGTruth++, a 10x increase. Hallucination rate jumps from 15.9% to 74.75% for the same 408 responses. This suggests that any model benchmarked only on original RAGTruth labels is evaluated against a substantially incomplete gold standard. Limitations per Blue Guardrails: (1) high hallucination density may create base-rate fallacy for detectors; (2) responses from 2023-era models may be too easy for current LLMs; (3) no data-to-text subset. Dataset viewer has a reported column mismatch bug (CSV format issue) as of May 2026. No formal arxiv paper found — published only as a HuggingFace dataset + blog post by a commercial AI safety startup.
public_code_url: [uncertain] — no separate code repository found. Annotation methodology described in blog post only.
gpu_requirements: N/A for the dataset itself — evaluation only benchmark. GPU requirements depend on the detection model being evaluated.
sample_size_for_replication: 408 (full dataset is the test set — no separate train/val splits reported)
min_samples_for_replication: 408 — the entire dataset is a single evaluation set; no subsampling needed for pilot
runtime_estimate: Very fast evaluation: 408 short responses (same length distribution as RAGTruth QA/summarization). Automated detection pass takes seconds-to-minutes depending on model.
cumulative_state_modeling: N/A — single-turn RAG responses, no multi-step trajectory
